items.name: createApp
items.description: Creates an application instance — the central object for registering middleware, routes, and settings. Supports set/get, enable/disable, locals, param handlers, groups, chaining, and route introspection.
tips: All route methods (get, post, etc.) return the app instance, allowing chaining.
app.set('key', value) and app.get('key') share a dual-purpose API like Express.
The { secure: true } route option rejects non-HTTPS requests with 403.

methods.description: Register middleware or mount a Router at a prefix. Runs in registration order.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Optional mount path prefix. Applies to all requests when omitted.
Middleware functions `(req, res, next) => void`.

methods.description: Register a GET route handler. Also: app.get(key) returns an app setting.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodOptions.notes: When true, matches HTTPS only; when false, matches HTTP only. Omit to match both.

methodParams.notes: Route path pattern. Supports `:param` names, `*` wildcards, and RegExp.
Per-route options — `timeout` (ms), `caseSensitive` (boolean), `strict` (boolean).
One or more `(req, res, next) => void` handlers. Executed left-to-right; call `next()` to continue.

methods.description: Register a POST route handler.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodOptions.notes: When true, matches HTTPS only; when false, matches HTTP only. Omit to match both.

methodParams.notes: Route path pattern. Supports `:param` names, `*` wildcards, and RegExp.
Per-route options — `timeout` (ms), `caseSensitive` (boolean), `strict` (boolean).
One or more `(req, res, next) => void` handlers. Executed left-to-right; call `next()` to continue.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodOptions.notes: When true, matches HTTPS only; when false, matches HTTP only. Omit to match both.

methodParams.notes: Route path pattern. Supports `:param` names, `*` wildcards, and RegExp.
Per-route options — `timeout` (ms), `caseSensitive` (boolean), `strict` (boolean).
One or more `(req, res, next) => void` handlers. Executed left-to-right; call `next()` to continue.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodOptions.notes: When true, matches HTTPS only; when false, matches HTTP only. Omit to match both.

methodParams.notes: Route path pattern. Supports `:param` names, `*` wildcards, and RegExp.
Per-route options — `timeout` (ms), `caseSensitive` (boolean), `strict` (boolean).
One or more `(req, res, next) => void` handlers. Executed left-to-right; call `next()` to continue.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodOptions.notes: When true, matches HTTPS only; when false, matches HTTP only. Omit to match both.

methodParams.notes: Route path pattern. Supports `:param` names, `*` wildcards, and RegExp.
Per-route options — `timeout` (ms), `caseSensitive` (boolean), `strict` (boolean).
One or more `(req, res, next) => void` handlers. Executed left-to-right; call `next()` to continue.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodOptions.notes: When true, matches HTTPS only; when false, matches HTTP only. Omit to match both.

methodParams.notes: Route path pattern. Supports `:param` names, `*` wildcards, and RegExp.
Per-route options — `timeout` (ms), `caseSensitive` (boolean), `strict` (boolean).
One or more `(req, res, next) => void` handlers. Executed left-to-right; call `next()` to continue.

methods.description: Register a HEAD route handler.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodOptions.notes: When true, matches HTTPS only; when false, matches HTTP only. Omit to match both.

methodParams.notes: Route path pattern. Supports `:param` names, `*` wildcards, and RegExp.
Per-route options — `timeout` (ms), `caseSensitive` (boolean), `strict` (boolean).
One or more `(req, res, next) => void` handlers. Executed left-to-right; call `next()` to continue.

methods.description: Register a handler for ALL HTTP methods.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodOptions.notes: When true, matches HTTPS only; when false, matches HTTP only. Omit to match both.

methodParams.notes: Route path pattern. Supports `:param` names, `*` wildcards, and RegExp.
Per-route options — `timeout` (ms), `caseSensitive` (boolean), `strict` (boolean).
One or more `(req, res, next) => void` handlers. Executed left-to-right; call `next()` to continue.

methods.description: Register a WebSocket upgrade handler. See Real-Time → WebSocket.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodOptions.notes: Maximum incoming WebSocket frame size in bytes.
Auto-ping interval in ms. Set 0 to disable.
(req) => boolean — return false to reject the upgrade.

methodParams.notes: WebSocket upgrade path.
Upgrade options — `timeout`, `maxPayload` (bytes), `perMessageDeflate` (boolean).
`(ws, req) => void` called once per connection.

methods.description: Register a parameter handler that fires when :name appears in a route.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Route parameter name (without `:`).
`(req, res, next, value, name) => void` — invoked whenever the named param is present.

methods.description: Group routes under a prefix with shared middleware.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodParams.notes: URL prefix applied to every route defined inside the group.
Optional middleware to apply to all routes in the group.
`(router) => void` callback that registers grouped routes.

methods.description: Start a route chain for a single path. Returns { get, post, put, delete, ... }.

methodReturns.description: A chainable helper for registering multiple HTTP methods on the same path.

methodParams.notes: Path to scope the chain to. Returns a chainable builder for attaching multiple HTTP-method handlers.

methods.description: Return the full route table for introspection/debugging.

methodReturns.description: Array of registered routes, each with { method, path, handlers }.

methods.description: Register a global error handler: (err, req, res, next) => {}.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodParams.notes: `(err, req, res) => void` — receives every error thrown or passed to `next(err)`.

methods.description: Set an application setting (also used as get(key) to retrieve).

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Setting name — e.g. `"trust proxy"`, `"case sensitive routing"`, `"strict routing"`, `"x-powered-by"`, `"etag"`.
Setting value.

methods.description: Set a boolean setting to true.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Boolean setting key, e.g. `"trust proxy"`, `"etag"`, `"x-powered-by"`, `"case sensitive routing"`.

methods.description: Set a boolean setting to false.

methodReturns.description: Returns the app instance for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Boolean setting key, e.g. `"trust proxy"`, `"etag"`, `"x-powered-by"`, `"case sensitive routing"`.

methods.description: Check if a setting is truthy.

methodReturns.description: true if the named setting is enabled.

methodParams.notes: Boolean setting key, e.g. `"trust proxy"`, `"etag"`, `"x-powered-by"`, `"case sensitive routing"`.

methods.description: Check if a setting is falsy.

methodReturns.description: true if the named setting is disabled.

methodParams.notes: Boolean setting key, e.g. `"trust proxy"`, `"etag"`, `"x-powered-by"`, `"case sensitive routing"`.

methods.description: A plain object for storing application-wide data. Merged into req.locals on each request.

methodReturns.description: Shared object available to all route handlers via req.locals and res.locals.

methods.description: Start the HTTP(S) server. Pass TLS options for HTTPS.

methodReturns.description: The underlying Node.js HTTP(S) Server instance.

methodOptions.notes: TLS private key (PEM). Enables HTTPS.
TLS certificate chain (PEM).
Optional CA certificate for mutual TLS.
PFX/PKCS#12 archive (alternative to key/cert).
Passphrase for the private key or PFX.

methodParams.notes: TCP port to bind. `0` picks a random available port.
TLS options `{ key, cert, ca }`. Presence switches to HTTPS/HTTP2.
`() => void` callback invoked once the server is listening.

methods.description: Shut down the server.

methodReturns.description: Closes the server and stops accepting new connections.

methodParams.notes: Optional callback invoked once the server finishes closing all connections.

methods.description: The raw (req, res) handler for use with custom HTTP servers.

methodReturns.description: Raw Node.js (req, res) handler — useful for embedding into an existing server.

items.name: Router
items.description: Creates a standalone modular router instance for organizing routes into sub-apps. Mount routers with app.use(prefix, router). Supports all the same route methods as createApp, plus route chaining and introspection.
items.example:
const { createApp, Router, json } = require('zero-http')
const app = createApp()
app.use(json())

const users = Router()
users.get('/', (req, res) => res.json([]))
users.get('/:id', (req, res) => res.json({ id: req.params.id }))
users.post('/', (req, res) => res.status(201).json(req.body))

const posts = Router()
posts.route('/')
	.get((req, res) => res.json([]))
	.post((req, res) => res.status(201).json(req.body))
posts.get('/:id', (req, res) => res.json({ id: req.params.id }))

app.use('/api/users', users)
app.use('/api/posts', posts)

app.get('/debug/routes', (req, res) => res.json(app.routes()))

app.listen(3000)
tips: Routers can be nested — mount a Router inside another Router.
Use app.routes() to see the full merged route table for debugging.
The { secure } route option works on router-level routes too.

methods.description: Register a GET route on this router.

methodReturns.description: Returns the router instance for chaining.

methodOptions.notes: When true, matches HTTPS only; when false, HTTP only. Omit to match both.

methodParams.notes: Route path pattern. Supports `:param` names, `*` wildcards, and RegExp.
Per-route options — `timeout` (ms), `caseSensitive` (boolean), `strict` (boolean).
One or more `(req, res, next) => void` handlers. Executed left-to-right; call `next()` to continue.

methods.description: Mount middleware or a nested router.

methodReturns.description: Returns the router instance for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Mount path prefix (optional).
Middleware functions.

methods.description: Create a route chain: router.route('/items').get(fn).post(fn).

methodReturns.description: Chainable helper scoped to the given path.

methodParams.notes: Route path. Returns a chainable builder with `.get()`, `.post()`, etc.

methods.description: Return the route table for this router.

methodReturns.description: All routes registered on this router.

items.name: Request
items.description: The request object wraps Node's IncomingMessage with Express-compatible properties and helpers. Available as the first argument to every route handler and middleware.
tips: req.query is always an object — even if no query string is present, it defaults to {}.
req.body is undefined until a body-parsing middleware populates it.
req.locals persists for the life of the request — use it to pass data between middleware.

methods.description: HTTP method string ('GET', 'POST', etc.).

methodReturns.description: HTTP method of the request (e.g. `GET`, `POST`, `PUT`).

methods.description: Full URL including query string.

methodReturns.description: Full request URL string including path and query string.

methods.description: URL path without query string.

methodReturns.description: Request pathname without query string (e.g. `/users/42`).

methods.description: Original URL as received — never rewritten by middleware.

methodReturns.description: The original, unmodified URL before any router base-path stripping.

methods.description: The URL prefix on which the current router was mounted.

methodReturns.description: URL prefix matched by the parent router. Empty string on the root router.

methods.description: Lower-cased request headers object.

methodReturns.description: Incoming request headers as a plain `{ name: value }` object.  Header names are lower-cased.

methods.description: Get a request header (case-insensitive).

methodReturns.description: Value of the named request header, or undefined if absent.

methodParams.notes: Header name (case-insensitive). `"Referrer"` and `"Referer"` are interchangeable.

methods.description: Check if Content-Type matches (e.g. req.is('json')).

methodReturns.description: true if the Content-Type matches the given type.

methodParams.notes: MIME type(s) to test against Content-Type, e.g. `"json"`, `"application/json"`, `["json","html"]`.

methods.description: Content negotiation — which types the client accepts.

methodReturns.description: Best matching Accept type from the request, or false if none match.

methodParams.notes: Content types in preference order, e.g. `"json"`, `"html"`, `"application/*"`. Returns best match or `false`.

methodReturns.description: Shorthand for req.headers['content-type'] — returns the request Content-Type MIME type, e.g. "application/json", "text/html".

methods.description: Route parameters from path segments (e.g. /:id).

methodReturns.description: Named route parameters extracted from the URL pattern (e.g. `{ id: "42" }` for `/users/:id`).

methods.description: Parsed query-string key/value pairs.

methodReturns.description: Parsed query-string parameters (e.g. `{ page: "2" }` for `?page=2`).

methods.description: Request body (populated by body-parsing middleware).

methodReturns.description: Parsed request body.  Value depends on the body-parser middleware applied — object for JSON/urlencoded, Buffer for raw, string for text, or `{ fields, files }` for multipart.

methods.description: Parsed cookies (populated by cookieParser).

methodReturns.description: Parsed cookie map `{ name: value }` populated by `cookieParser()` middleware.

methods.description: Verified signed cookies (populated by cookieParser with a secret).

methodReturns.description: Signed cookies that passed HMAC verification, keyed by cookie name.

methods.description: First signing secret (set by cookieParser). Used by res.cookie({ signed: true }).

methodReturns.description: The cookie signing secret(s) set by `cookieParser(secret)`.

methods.description: Remote IP address.

methodReturns.description: Remote client IP address.  Trusts `X-Forwarded-For` when the `trustProxy` app setting is enabled.

methods.description: true if the connection is over TLS.

methodReturns.description: true if the request was made over HTTPS.

methods.description: 'https' or 'http'.

methodReturns.description: `"https"` or `"http"` depending on the connection type.

methods.description: Hostname from the Host header.

methodReturns.description: Hostname derived from the `Host` request header.

methods.description: Array of subdomains (offset defaults to 2).

methodReturns.description: Array of subdomain segments, reversed. E.g. "a.b.example.com" → ["b","a"].

methods.description: true if X-Requested-With is XMLHttpRequest.

methodReturns.description: true if the X-Requested-With header is "XMLHttpRequest".

methods.description: true if the client cache is still valid (ETag/Last-Modified).

methodReturns.description: true if the response is considered fresh (ETag / Last-Modified match).

methods.description: Inverse of fresh.

methodReturns.description: true if the response is considered stale.

methods.description: Request-scoped data store (merged from app.locals).

methodReturns.description: Request-scoped storage object for passing data between middleware and route handlers.  Defaults to `{}`.

methods.description: Unique request ID (set by requestId middleware).

methodReturns.description: Unique request ID attached by `requestId()` middleware.

methods.description: Parse the Range header. Returns object or -1/-2.

methodReturns.description: Parsed byte-ranges array, -1 for unsatisfiable, or -2 for invalid syntax.

methodParams.notes: Total resource size in bytes. Used to validate and clamp the incoming `Range` header.

methods.description: The original Node IncomingMessage.

methodReturns.description: The underlying Node.js `http.IncomingMessage` object for low-level access.

items.name: Response
items.description: The response object wraps Node's ServerResponse with chainable methods for setting status codes, headers, cookies, and sending various response types. Available as the second argument to every route handler.

options.notes: Restrict cookie to HTTP(S) only — no JavaScript access.
Auto-sign the cookie value using req.secret (set by cookieParser).
CHIPS partitioned attribute. Requires secure: true. Isolates cookies per top-level site.

tips: res.cookie() with signed: true requires cookieParser to be configured with a secret.
Object values passed to res.cookie() are auto-serialized with the j: prefix for JSON cookies.
res.send(object) calls res.json() automatically — you can use either interchangeably.

methods.description: Set the HTTP status code. Chainable.

methodReturns.description: Returns the response object for chaining.

methods.description: Set a response header. Chainable.

methodReturns.description: Returns the response object for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Header name, or an `{ name: value }` object to set multiple headers at once.
Header value (required when `name` is a string). Array sets multiple values.

methods.description: Get a previously-set response header.

methodReturns.description: Current value of the named response header.

methodParams.notes: Header name to retrieve (case-insensitive).

methods.description: Append a value to a header.

methodReturns.description: Returns the response object for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Header name.
Value to append (existing values are preserved).

methods.description: Add a field to the Vary header.

methodReturns.description: Returns the response object for chaining.

methodParams.notes: Field to add to the `Vary` header, e.g. `"Accept"`, `"Accept-Encoding"`.

methods.description: Set Content-Type. Chainable.

methodReturns.description: Returns the response object for chaining.

methodParams.notes: MIME type or file extension, e.g. `"json"`, `"html"`, `"text/plain"`, `".png"`. Extensions are resolved via mime-db.

methods.description: Send a response (string, Buffer, object, or null). Auto-sets Content-Type.

methodReturns.description: Sends the response and ends the connection.

methodParams.notes: Response body. Objects are JSON-serialized automatically.

methods.description: Send a JSON response with Content-Type: application/json.

methodReturns.description: Sends the response and ends the connection.

methodParams.notes: Value serialized as JSON. Sets `Content-Type: application/json`.

methods.description: Send a plain text response.

methodReturns.description: Sends the response and ends the connection.

methodParams.notes: Plain text string. Sets `Content-Type: text/plain`.

methods.description: Send an HTML response.

methodReturns.description: Sends the response and ends the connection.

methodParams.notes: HTML string. Sets `Content-Type: text/html`.

methods.description: Send only the status code with its reason phrase as body.

methodReturns.description: Sends the response and ends the connection.

methodParams.notes: HTTP status code. Sets the status and sends the standard phrase as the body (e.g. `200` → `"OK"`).

methods.description: Stream a file as the response with appropriate Content-Type.

methodReturns.description: Sends the response and ends the connection.

methodOptions.notes: Root directory to resolve the path from.
Cache-Control max-age in ms.
Additional response headers to set.
"allow", "deny", or "ignore" for dot-prefixed files.

methodParams.notes: Absolute path or relative to the `root` option.
File-send options.
`(err) => void` — invoked when done or on error.

methods.description: Prompt a file download with Content-Disposition header.

methodReturns.description: Sends the response and ends the connection.

methodParams.notes: File path to stream as a download.
Override filename shown in the browser's save dialog.
`(err) => void` invoked on completion or error.

methods.description: Send a redirect response. Default status: 302.

methodReturns.description: Sends the response and ends the connection.

methodParams.notes: Redirect status code. Defaults to `302`. Use `301` for permanent.
Redirect target (absolute or relative URL).

methods.description: Content negotiation — respond based on Accept header. Keys are MIME types.

methodReturns.description: Sends the response and ends the connection.

methodParams.notes: Object keyed by MIME type with response handler callbacks. The best `Accept` match is selected. Include a `default` key as fallback.

methods.description: Set a cookie. Supports signed (auto-sign via req.secret), priority (Low/Medium/High), partitioned (CHIPS), and auto-serializes objects as JSON cookies (j: prefix). Chainable.

methodReturns.description: Returns the response object for chaining.

methodOptions.notes: Cookie lifetime in milliseconds.
Explicit expiry date.
Cookie path scope.
Cookie domain scope.
Send only over HTTPS.
Prevent JavaScript access to the cookie.
"Strict", "Lax", or "None".
Sign the value using req.secret.

methodParams.notes: Cookie name.
Cookie value. Objects are JSON-stringified.
Cookie attributes.

methods.description: Clear a cookie by setting it to expire. Chainable.

methodReturns.description: Returns the response object for chaining.

methodOptions.notes: Must match the path used when the cookie was set.
Must match the domain used when the cookie was set.

methodParams.notes: Cookie name to clear.
Must match the original cookie's `path` and `domain` to clear correctly.

methods.description: Set the Link header from { rel: url } pairs. Chainable.

methodReturns.description: Returns the response object for chaining.

methodParams.notes: `{ rel: url }` pairs appended to the `Link` response header, e.g. `{ next: "/page/2", prev: "/page/0" }`.

methods.description: Set the Location header. Chainable.

methodReturns.description: Returns the response object for chaining.

methodParams.notes: URL to set in the `Location` header. Use `"back"` to redirect to the `Referer`.

methods.description: Open a Server-Sent Events stream. See Real-Time → SSE.

methodReturns.description: A new SSE stream attached to the response.

methodOptions.notes: Auto keep-alive ping interval in ms. Set 0 to disable.
Client reconnect time in ms sent on connection.
Enable response compression.

methodParams.notes: SSE stream options.

methods.description: true if headers have already been sent.

methodReturns.description: true if response headers have already been sent.

methods.description: Request-scoped data store.

methodReturns.description: Response-scoped storage shared with the request locals object.

methods.description: The original Node ServerResponse.

methodReturns.description: The underlying Node.js `http.ServerResponse` for low-level write access.
